sections.paragraph:
- Original: αὐτός

- Transliteration: Autos

- Phonetic: ow-tos'

- Definition:

1. himself, herself, themselves, itself

2. he, she, it

3. the same

- Origin: from the particle au [perhaps akin to the base of G109 through the idea of a baffling wind] (backward)

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech:

- Strong's: From the particle αὖ au (perhaps akin to the base of G109 through the idea of a baffling wind; backward); the reflexive pronoun self used (alone or in the compound of G1438) of the third person and (with the proper personal pronoun) of the other persons: - her it (-self) one the other (mine) own said ([self-] the) same ([him- my- thy-]) self [your-] selves she that their (-s) them ([-selves]) there [-at -by -in -into -of -on -with] they (these) things this (man) those together very which. Compare G848.
